segments.text: The Lord told Moses, {
Tell Aaron's sons, the priests: A priest is not to make himself unclean by touching the dead body of any of his relatives.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: The only exceptions are for his immediate family. This includes his mother, father, son, daughter, or brother,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: or his unmarried sister since she is a close relative because she doesn't have a husband.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He must not make himself unclean for those only related to him by marriage—he is not to make himself ceremonially impure.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Priests are not to shave bald spots on their heads, trim the sides of their beards, or cut their bodies.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They must be holy to their God and not disgrace their God's reputation. They are the ones who present the food offerings to the Lord, the food of their God. Consequently they must be holy.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: A priest is not to marry a woman made unclean through prostitution or who is divorced by her husband, for the priest must be holy to his God.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: You shall consider him holy because he presents the food offerings to your God. He shall be holy to you, because I am holy. I am the Lord, and I chose you as my special people.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: A priest's daughter who makes herself unclean through prostitution makes her father unclean. She must be executed by burning.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The high priest has the highest place among the other priests. He has been anointed with olive oil poured on his head and has been ordained to wear the priestly clothing. He must not leave his hair uncombed or tear his clothes.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He is not to go near any dead body. He must not make himself unclean, even if it is for his own father or mother.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He must not leave to deal with someone who has died or make the sanctuary of his God unclean because he has been dedicated by the anointing oil of his God. I am the Lord.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He can only marry a virgin.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He must not marry a widow, a divorced woman, or one made unclean through prostitution. He has to marry a virgin from his own people,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: so that he doesn't make his children unclean among his people,  for I am the Lord who makes him holy.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The Lord told Moses,
Tell Aaron: These rules apply for all future generations. If any of your descendants has a physical defect, he is not allowed to come and present the food offerings of his God.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: No man is allowed to do this if he has any defects, including anyone who is blind, crippled, facially disfigured, or has deformities,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: anyone who has a broken foot or arm,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: anyone who is a hunchback or dwarf, or who has cataracts, skin sores or scabs, or a damaged testicle.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: No descendant of Aaron the priest who has a defect is allowed to come and present the food offerings to the Lord. Because he has a defect, he must not come and offer the food of his God.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He is still allowed to eat the food from the Most Holy Place of his God and also from the sanctuary,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: but because he has a defect, he is not allowed to go near the curtain or the altar, so that my sanctuary and everything in it are not made unclean, because I am the Lord who makes them holy.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Moses repeated this to Aaron and his sons, and to all the Israelites.
